rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK HUTCHINS MOLL VS. JUDITH M BRASS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT JUDITH BRASS' OBJECTIONS TO PROPOSED JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 26, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT JUDITH BRASS' OBJECTIONS TO PROPOSED JUDGMENT Hearing Required. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CANDLESTICK R.V. PARK VS. MARIA JIMENEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Directing The Clerk To Enter Judgment For Possession, Or In The Alternative, For An Order For Entry Of Judgment And Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 26, 2025 line 6. PLAINTIFF CANDLESTICK R.V. PARK A CALIFORNIA STOCK CORPORATION MOTION FOR ORDER DIRECTING THE CLERK TO ENTER JUDGMENT FOR POSSESSION, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR AN ORDER FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND JUDGMENT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Plaintiff's Motion for Order Directing the Clerk to Enter Judgment for Possession is DENIED. Plaintiff's complaint purports to be a complaint for unlawful detainer seeking possession and damages. Plaintiff alleges to be the owner of the mobilehome park. TPA 2019 defines "Residential real property" as any dwelling or unit that is intended for human habitation, including any dwelling or unit in a mobilehome park. Plaintiff's complaint does not allege compliance with or exemption from TPA 2019. Plaintiff is not required to use the optional UD form complaint but should use it for guidance as to the allegations deemed appropriate to be included in the complaint for unlawful detainer by the Judicial Council. Under the circumstances, in an unlawful detainer case seeking possession and damages for the premises located in a mobilepark, the clerks have no authority to enter a default judgement in the absence of basic elements of the complaint. However, unlike the clerks, the Court is not limited to performing ministerial duties and may interpret allegations in the complaint, which in later paragraphs discusses a rental agreement for a lot and references CC 799.70 pertaining to Recreational Vehicle Park (as opposed to mobilehome park). In light of all allegations in the Complaint, the inference is that TPA 2019 does not apply to this leasehold, a determination that cannot be made by the clerks. Therefore, Plaintiff's request for a Court judgement for possession is granted. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
